articles.expand: Societe Generale analysts highlight that stronger‑than‑forecast United Kingdom (UK) inflation and PMIs (Purchasing Managers' Index) raise the question of a potential hawkish dissent at next week’s Bank of England (BoE) meeting, even though the house view is for a unanimous hold.

articles.title: The USD is lower to kickstart Friday'trade. Iran sends delegation to Pakistan
articles.expand: The USD is softer heading into Friday trade, with the greenback slipping to fresh session lows as North American desks come in. Risk sentiment is getting a modest boost from headlines that Iran will send a delegation to Pakistan this weekend, helping lift equities after yesterday’s dip. On the corporate side, Intel is the standout. Shares are sharp...

articles.title: Volatile etf flows signal investor caution in the gold market
articles.expand: Gold markets experienced strong volatility in Q1, with record highs followed by sharp corrections. ETF flows reflected investor caution, shifting from strong inflows to outflows by March. High prices are dampening jewelry demand, while central bank actions add complexity. Despite weaker physical demand, geopolitical risks and expectations of looser...

articles.title: ECB: Rate hike call pushed to June – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior Macro Strategist Bas van Geffen argues that muted market reactions have reduced urgency for central banks to act, even as Middle East conflict risks persist.

articles.title: GBP: Retail surprise tempers Pound outlook – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities analysts note United Kingdom (UK) Retail Sales for March surprised to the upside at 0.7% month-on-month versus their 0.1% forecast and a flat market consensus. The strength was driven mainly by food and fuel, with Easter timing and front-loaded fuel purchases.

articles.title: EUR/JPY edges higher as policy caution, Middle East war temper Yen strength
articles.expand: EUR/JPY trades around 186.70 on Friday, posting a modest 0.06% gain as investors remain cautious ahead of a week marked by monetary policy decisions from several major central banks. The pair is supported by a broadly resilient Euro (EUR), while facing mixed flows on the Japanese Yen (JPY).

articles.title: USD/CHF eases from daily highs, holds 0.7860 following comments by SNB Schlegel
articles.expand: The US Dollar (USD) maintains a near-term bullish trend against the Swiss Franc (CHF), but the pair eased from 10-day highs of 0.7875 on Friday to levels close to 0.7860, as the President of the Swiss National Bank (SNB), Martin Schlegel, hinted at changes in the bank’s monetary policy.

articles.title: DXY: Fed repricing supports US currency – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank analysts note that strong United States (US) Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) data have reinforced perceptions of economic resilience and rising price pressures, leading markets to scale back expectations for Federal Reserve (Fed) rate cuts.

articles.title: ECB: Hawkish hold and June options – Nordea
articles.expand: Nordea’s Chief Analyst Jan von Gerich expects the European Central Bank (ECB) to leave policy rates unchanged at the April meeting while keeping all options open for June. He anticipates a hawkish communication tone that underpins market expectations for a June hike.
